summary: Iter23 Stripe Coupon Codes + generic Login refresh tested end-to-end. Backend: 23/23 iter23 tests PASS + 34/34 iter20-22 regression PASS + 15/15 iter19 regression PASS = 72/72 backend tests PASS. Frontend: Login page shows new tagline 'Bot de WhatsApp con IA para tu negocio' (old text removed), /logo-generic.svg loads (HTTP 200, image/svg+xml, naturalWidth=150). /config Programa de referidos shows purple promo card with code 'DEMOIN-G8AUAA' in monospace font ('SF Mono', Menlo, monospace) + functional copy button. Referral link with UTM still works (regression OK).
critical_code_review_comments: get_or_create_referral_code Stripe block correctly handles missing STRIPE_API_KEY by skipping silently and returning stripe_enabled=false (graceful).
Promo card UI uses monospace + tracking; copy button gracefully degrades when Clipboard API blocked (logs to console, no crash).
Note: navigator.clipboard fails in headless/non-secure contexts but works in user browsers — consider fallback (textarea + execCommand) for older browsers, but low priority.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing demo-inmobiliaria seed. Demo tenant code persisted: DEMOIN-G8AUAA. All test data cleaned up via cleanup_tracker fixture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter23 fully validated. STRIPE_API_KEY not set in preview → stripe_enabled=false expected. Real Stripe webhook attribution via promo metadata is unit-tested with mocks (PromotionCode.retrieve). When STRIPE_API_KEY is added in production, lazy creation of Coupon + PromotionCode will trigger on first GET /api/commissions/promo-code per tenant. Frontend served via 'serve -s build' — main agent must rebuild frontend after JS/CSS changes.
